jd_text: Digital Content Assistant Manager – Art Dubai, Dubai Collection The Digital Content Assistant Manager supports Art Dubai and Dubai Collection by creating and managing digital content across all platforms. This is a hands-on role requiring the ability to manage monthly content calendars, support creative briefs, coordinates stakeholder outreach, and ensure well-organised digital assets. The role also requires the ability to create content across photography, video, graphic design, and written formats for fairs, events, and year-round programming, as well as support live content capture during key moments such as fair weeks. Key Responsibilities: Art Dubai Manage digital platforms and social media channels, including liaising with the Communications Manager and Marketing Manager. Manage photography and videography for the fair and its year-round programming. Produce and manage fair and year-round content and assets across video and photography. Track and report on digital performance across channels, including Art Dubai and its partners on social media. Develop and manage a monthly content calendar, including production of visual assets and copy. Support sponsor communications, including on-site content capture. Coordinate outreach and communication with galleries, exhibitors, and artists in relation to content. Manage live content capture during fair week. Support the development and execution of an integrated content strategy with the Communications Manager and Marketing Manager to create an omnichannel approach. Sustainably grow digital audience sizes and increase engagement volume as per agreed strategy. Oversee community engagement across social media by managing interactions, responding to comments and messages, and actively participating in conversations in a timely and thoughtful manner. Foster strong relationships with followers to build trust, encourage interaction, and grow a loyal, engaged audience. Dubai Collection Manage digital channels and social media channels. Oversee website content and structure in collaboration with the Head of Collection Management. Coordinate with Marketing on website updates and campaigns. Manage photography and videography for year-round programming and events. Manage creative brief development for year-round content and assets Produce and manage year-round content and assets across video, photography, and graphic design. Track and report on performance across all channels Develop and manage a monthly content calendar, including production of visual assets and copy Manage outreach to patrons and artists for content development. Manage live content capture during events. Assist with development and execution of overall Content Strategy with the Communications Manager and Marketing Manager to create an omnichannel approach. Drive audience growth and engagement volume as per agreed strategy Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Digital Media, or a related field. 3-5 years of experience in content production, social media coordination, and digital marketing, preferably within the creative industries, events, or cultural sectors. Strong understanding of social media platforms and platform-specific best practices. Experience collaborating with external creative teams, including photographers, videographers, and content agencies. Strong copywriting and editing skills with a good eye for visual storytelling and design alignment. Proficiency in social media and analytics tools (e.g., Meta Business Suite, Later, Sprout, Google Analytics). Familiarity with supporting digital advertising campaigns across Meta, Google, and LinkedIn. Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. Creative, collaborative, and proactive approach with strong attention to detail. Familiarity with design tools (e.g., Canva, Adobe Creative Suite). Basic video or image editing skills and comfort with adapting content formats for social platforms is an advantage. How to apply Please submit your CV via this job posting. Please note that this is an on-site role requiring office attendance five days a week.
